title: eclipta erecta
intro: Eclipta erecta, also known as false daisy or bhringaraj, is a flowering plant native to Asia and North America, characterized by its yellow daisy-like flowers and slender stem. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of this plant have been used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Eclipta erecta include promoting hair growth, reducing stress and anxiety, and supporting liver function, as well as aiding in the treatment of fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues. Eclipta erecta can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and oils, to harness its medicinal properties.
